talk_title: TAP2 downregulation drives immune evasion and can be therapeutically reversed

speaker.name: Kurt Sch"alper
speaker.affiliation: University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center, Department of Oncology

targets.dependency_context: lung cancer cells (KRAS or EGFR mutant)
acquired resistance to dual checkpoint blockade
myeloid-derived suppressor cells in lung tumor microenvironment
lung and pancreatic tumors with immune exclusion phenotype

biomarkers.name: TAP2 low expression
biomarkers.finding: Patients with low TAP2 had a hazard ratio of 1.8 for death after immunotherapy (n≈819, retrospective cohort)

biomarkers.name: IL4 RNA in tumor microenvironment
biomarkers.finding: IL4 RNA colocalized with CD11b+ myeloid cells in TAP2-low tumor regions

biomarkers.name: SOX1 upregulation
biomarkers.finding: TAP2 knockdown increased SOX1 protein levels; overexpression of TAP2 reduced SOX1

models.name: 819 treatment‑naïve lung tumors
models.genotype_or_key_features: baseline specimens, diverse histology, prior to any therapy

models.name: KRAS‑mutant and EGFR‑mutant lung cancer lines
primary human lung tumors (n≈250) for spatial transcriptomics
Tumor‑Associated Primary Explants (TAPEs)
models.genotype_or_key_features: patient‑derived lung tumors cultured up to 10 days

clinical_data.population: low TAP2 lung cancer patients treated with immunotherapy

MOA_summary: Downregulation of TAP2 in lung cancer cells reduces antigen presentation modestly (10‑15% peptide loss) but more critically impairs interferon‑γ signaling via upregulation of the inhibitor SOX1 and suppression of chemokines (CXCL10, CCL5, CXCL11), leading to T‑cell exclusion. IL‑4 produced by tumor‑associated myeloid cells epigenetically silences TAP2, an effect reversible by IL‑4R blockade or epigenetic drugs (HDAC inhibitors, taxanes). Restoring TAP2 re‑sensitizes cells to cytokine signaling and tumor‑antigen specific killing. Separately, secreted factors such as PLA2G10 act as active T‑cell excluders, limiting lymphocyte migration and promoting tumor growth; blocking these molecules restores T‑cell infiltration.
key_findings: 24% of treatment‑naïve lung cancers show cancer‑cell selective TAP2 downregulation; only 6% show TAP1 loss.
Low TAP2 expression predicts an 80% increased risk of death (HR 1.8) after immunotherapy, with no effect on chemotherapy outcomes.
TAP2 knockout reduces tumor‑antigen specific CD8 T‑cell killing more than TAP1 loss; re‑expression of TAP2 rescues killing.
TAP2 loss leads to a 10‑15% reduction in total HLA‑bound peptides but causes a >50% decrease in interferon‑γ‑induced gene expression (e.g., IDO1, PD‑L1) via SOX1 up‑regulation.
IL‑4 secreted by CD11b+ myeloid cells epigenetically closes the TAP2 promoter, an effect not reversed by exogenous IFN‑γ/TNF‑α.
HDAC inhibitors (vorinostat), taxanes, and dasatinib up‑regulate TAP2 without increasing PD‑L1, restoring antigen presentation and T‑cell killing in vitro.
Secreted phospholipase PLA2G10 acts as a T‑cell excluder; overexpression in mouse models accelerates tumor growth and reduces CD8 infiltration, while anti‑PLA2G10 antibody restores T‑cell entry.
Spatial analysis of 250 lung cancers identifies ~20% with a dominant immune‑excluded phenotype, which correlates with poorer immunotherapy survival.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: TAP2 loss confers resistance specifically to checkpoint blockade, not to chemotherapy.
IL‑4–mediated epigenetic silencing of TAP2 is not overcome by IFN‑γ/TNF‑α stimulation, indicating a durable resistance mechanism.

combination_strategies.rationale: Blocks IL‑4–driven epigenetic silencing of TAP2, restoring antigen presentation and enhancing PD‑1 blockade efficacy
combination_strategies.phenotype: In autologous co‑culture, combined IL‑4R blockade + pembrolizumab increased CD8 activation (CD25+) and cancer cell killing (Annexin V+ EpCAM+)

combination_strategies.rationale: Epigenetically re‑expresses TAP2 without up‑regulating PD‑L1
combination_strategies.phenotype: Increased TAP2 protein, surface peptide load, and tumor‑antigen specific T‑cell killing in vitro

combination_strategies.rationale: Induces TAP2 expression and inflammatory signaling
combination_strategies.phenotype: Up‑regulation of TAP2 and enhanced T‑cell mediated cytotoxicity

combination_strategies.rationale: Neutralizes secreted PLA2G10 to relieve active T‑cell exclusion
combination_strategies.phenotype: In mouse KPC and MC38 models, antibody treatment increased CD8 infiltration and synergized with anti‑PD‑1

open_questions: What upstream signals drive IL‑4 production by myeloid cells in TAP2‑low tumors?
Can plasma IL‑4 levels reliably predict TAP2 epigenetic silencing across cancer types?
What are the precise molecular mechanisms by which PLA2G10 impairs T‑cell migration?
Will TAP2‑restoring epigenetic therapies improve clinical outcomes when combined with checkpoint inhibitors in a prospective trial?
